description: Evaluation test cases for pptx-creation-editing skill

tests.description: Create a new .pptx with a title slide and a content slide containing an embedded image with alt text. Verify slide count, alt text, and notes are correct.
assertions: picture alt text == 'A vibrant orange square'
slides[1].notes == 'This slide contains an embedded image.'
file is a valid ZIP archive

tests.description: Open a newly created presentation, add speaker notes to slide index 0 using set_notes(), save, reload, and read back the notes text.
assertions: notes text == 'Welcome to the presentation. Please hold questions until the end.'

tests.description: Open SampleShow.pptx from the Apache POI corpus. Read all speaker notes and verify they match expected content.
assertions: notes[0] == 'I am the notes of the first slide'
notes[1] contains 'These are the notes of the 2nd slide'

tests.description: Create a 3-slide deck, move slide at index 2 to position 0, verify new order.
Open shapes.pptx from the POI corpus. On slide 0: replace the first picture with a new image (blue PNG). Add speaker notes 'Round-trip test complete.'. Save, reload, validate.
assertions: replaced image is present in media/ of the ZIP
blip r:embed points to new rId
notes text == 'Round-trip test complete.' on slide 0

tests.description: Generate a VBA Sub that loops over all shapes on slide 1 of the active presentation and sets the font color of any text frame to red (RGB 255, 0, 0). Save the code as a .bas module file using write_bas_file().
assertions: generated code contains For Each or For loop over Shapes
generated code references TextFrame or HasTextFrame
generated code sets font color (Font.Color or RGB)
.bas file starts with Attribute VB_Name header

tests.description: Generate a VBA Sub that adds a new slide at the end of the active presentation using a title-only layout, sets the title text to 'Generated Slide', and saves the presentation.
assertions: generated code calls Slides.Add or ActivePresentation.Slides.Add
generated code sets title text (Title.TextFrame or similar)
generated code calls Save or SaveAs

tests.description: Build the correct POWERPNT.exe command-line invocation to open a macro-enabled presentation at C:\Decks\report.pptm and run the macro named BuildSlides. Note: the /M switch syntax is POWERPNT.exe /M <file> "<MacroName>" — the file path comes immediately after /M, then the macro name as a separate argument.
assertions: macro name BuildSlides appears as a separate argument after the file path
